learn.core_idea: MLFQ approximates shortest-job preference by adapting priority based on observed CPU behavior.
mechanism_steps: New work starts at high priority.
Interactive-like behavior tends to keep priority.
Scheduler chooses highest non-empty queue first.
learn.worked_example: Short bursts get early service while long bursts still progress through lower queues.
common_mistakes: Treating MLFQ as fixed-priority static scheduling.
Ignoring that policy reacts to runtime behavior.
Assuming high-priority jobs always complete first.
pre_challenge_checklist: I can identify queue-biased dispatch patterns.
I can verify completion does not regress.
